steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The strongest clue is the final line “But Francie was a reader” following her admission that some B-books “had been hard going.” The “but” signals persistence despite difficulty: she continues her alphabetical reading plan despite slow progress (still in the B's after a long time) and acknowledged tedium.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
The correct response directly states what this pattern reveals: Francie's determination to meet her ambitious goal.

The others miss the main point—
  • A claims she enjoys unusual topics (contradicted by the difficulty acknowledged)
  • B invokes a preference never discussed
  • D focuses on a single book rather than her overall goal-driven behavior


That confirms C is correct.
